REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, PLAINTIFF LAMAR BURKS, JOHN SANDLES, CLEO BRAZIER, ANDRE SANDLES, SANDRENA SANDLES MOTION FOR POSSESSION OF REAL PROPERTY IS DENIED. MOVING PARTY FAILS TO CITE ANY AUTHORITY FOR THE PROPOSITION THAT REMOVAL OF THE CO-OWNER FROM THE PROPERTY MAY BE ACCOMPLISHED BY A MOTION FILED BY PLAINTIFFS. INTERLOCUTORY JUDGMENT VESTS THE POWER TO "EVICT OR FACILITATE THE EVICTION OF OWNER-OCCUPANTS AND/OR TENANTS" AND THE REFEREE SHALL PROCEED ACCORDINGLY. (INTERLOCUTORY JUDGMENT, PARAGRAPH 13(C)). PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER. JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: J. MURPHY; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

Motion For Possession Of Real Property
Off Calendar Jan-23-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: PLAINTIFF LAMAR BURKS, JOHN SANDLES, CLEO BRAZIER, ANDRE SANDLES, SANDRENA SANDLES Motion For Possession Of Real Property is DENIED. Moving party fails to cite any authority for the proposition that removal of the co-owner from the property may be accomplished by a motion filed by Plaintiffs. Interlocutory judgment vests the power to "evict or facilitate the eviction of owner-occupants and/or tenants" and the referee shall proceed accordingly. (Interlocutory Judgment, paragraph 13(c)). Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (D501)

MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Further Hearing on Entry of Proposed Interlocutory Judgment.
Dept. 514. Judge: Gerardo C. Sandoval. Clerk: Lyssette Bareng. Reporter: Giselle Casey, CSR #8098. Telephone: 415-551-3680. Email: gcasey@sftc.org.

No appearance by defendant Terence Leroy Sandles.

Default was entered on defendent Terence Leroy Sandles on November 6, 2019.

Prove-Up Motion
Off Calendar Oct-29-2020 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing as to defendant Terence Leroy Sandles.
Dept. 514. Judge: Gerardo C. Sandoval. Clerk: Lyssette Bareng. Reporter: Giselle Casey, CSR #8098. Telephone: 415-551-3680. Email: gcasey@sftc.org.

Ashlee Gonzales, Esq., appeared for plaintiffs via ZOOM Video Conference.
Plaintiff Lamar Burks, who appeared for this morning's proceedings via ZOOM Video Conference, was placed under oath and testified.
Plaintiffs John D. Sandles and Sandrena Sandles also appeared for this morning's proceedings via ZOOM Video Conference, were placed under oath but did not testify.
No appearance by defendant Terence Leroy Sandles.
Plaintiffs' Complaint to Quiet Title, for Partition by Sale, Accounting and Declaratory Relief was filed on September 18, 2019.
Summons, Complaint and accompanying documents were personally served on defendant Terence Leroy Sandles on September 18, 2019.
Notice of Pendency of Action (Lis Pendens) was filed on October 10, 2019
Default was entered on defendent Terence Leroy Sandles on November 6, 2019.
DOE defendants have not been dismissed.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of plaintiffs Lamar D. Burks, an individual; John D. Sandles, an individual; Cleo Lynell Brazier, an individual; Andre Sandles, an individual; and Sandrena Sandles, an individual, as follows:

(1) The Court Provisionally Appoints the Honorable Richard Flier (Retired) as Partition Referee.
(2) The Court Provisionally Approves the Sale of Real Property, commmonly known as 55 Jules Avenue, San Francisco, CA 94112.
(3) That parties individual interest in the Net Funds After Sale of the Real Property commonly known as 55 Jules Avenue, San Francisco, CA 94112 is:
(a) Lamar D. Burks, an individual (20% interest)
(b) John D. Sandles, an individual (20% interest)
(c) Cleo Lynell Brazier, an individual (20% interest)
(d) Andre Sandles, an individual (10% interest)
(e) Sandrena Sandles, an individual (10% interest)
(f) Terence Leroy Sandles (20% interest) - Defendant
(4) Expenditures to prepare the real property for sale shall not exceed $50,000.00;

Matter is CONTINUED pending submission of Grant Deed with attached Declaration from a Custodian of Record of the Real Property, and for Entry of Final Judgment.

Counsel for plaintiff shall prepare the proposed Interlocutory Judgment and submit same to the Court for review and signature.
